title: 10 best herbal tinctures for ear blockage
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for ear blockage are a natural solution to alleviate congestion and discomfort in the ears, promoting healthy ear function and hearing. They work by thinning mucus and reducing inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for ear blockage include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Hydrastis canadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Echinacea angustifolia, Thymus serpyllum, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, ear blockage can lead to chronic ear infections, tinnitus, and even hearing loss, significantly impacting one's quality of life, causing distress, discomfort, and isolation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The herbal extract contains compounds that help reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage of mucus and air to enter the middle ear. This can provide relief from pressure, discomfort, and pain associated with ear blockage. Additionally, lemon verbena's antibacterial properties may also help combat any underlying infections that could be contributing to the blockage.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Lemon verbena's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort in the ears, making it easier to alleviate congestion and ear blockages.

Expectorant and Decongestant Properties: The expectorant and decongestant properties of lemon verbena tincture help loosen and clear out excess mucus and fluids from the Eustachian tube, relieving pressure and discomfort in the ears.

Antioxidant and Immune-System Modulating Properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidant and immune-system modulating properties help boost the body's natural defense against infections and inflammation, reducing the risk of middle ear infections and promoting overall ear health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area, which can aid in relieving ear blockage.
Terpenes: Specifically, borneol, a terpene found in Verbena officinalis, may help thin mucus and reduce congestion in the ears, making it easier to breathe and reducing blockage.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties that may help combat ear infections and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to ear blockage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and clear ear blockages.
Roots: Employed for their expectorant and antiseptic properties, which aid in loosening and removing earwax and debris.
Flowers: Utilized for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that contribute to ear blockages.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers and dry them completely in a warm place.
Combine the dried herb with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar in a ratio.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow the infusion to mature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator to preserve its potency and use within 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the delicate skin inside the ear canal. The herb's natural compounds also have antimicrobial properties that help to eliminate excess wax and debris that can cause congestion and blockages. By reducing inflammation and clearing out impurities, pot marigold tinctures promote a healthy environment in the ear, allowing for proper drainage and relief from feelings of fullness or pressure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the ears caused by ear blockage.

Antibacterial properties: The tannins and saponins present in pot marigold have antibacterial properties, making it effective against bacterial infections that may cause ear blockage and related symptoms such as discharge or odor.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds can help protect the ears from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to chronic ear problems like blockage.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the ear by breaking down and dissolving the blockages, thereby relieving ear congestion.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Calendula possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat infection in the ear, making it easier to clear out blockages.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Calendula have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate bacteria and other pathogens that may be contributing to ear blockage, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are used to make tinctures for ear blockage due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and promote healing in the ear.
Stems: The stems contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in soothing and calming the ear canal.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when they are dry and free from moisture.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper bags for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it vigorously 2-3 times a day for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can effectively soothe irritated eustachian tubes and relieve pressure in the ears. The tincture's antiseptic compounds also help to reduce bacterial and viral infections that can cause congestion and discomfort in the ears. By promoting drainage and reducing swelling, Kansas coneflower tinctures provide natural relief from ear blockage and associated symptoms like popping, ringing, and decreased hearing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in Kansas Coneflower tincture reduce swelling and inflammation in the Eustachian tube, helping to alleviate ear blockage.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The bioactive compounds in Kansas Coneflighter tincture inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infection and congestion in the ears, contributing to blockage.

Soothing and analgesic properties: The essential oils and flavonoids in Kansas Coneflower tincture provide a calming and numbing effect, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with ear blockage.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and congestion in the ear, thereby alleviating blockage.

Alkaloids (specifically, isobutylamides and isobutylamides-derived compounds): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections that can cause ear blockage and promote healing in the affected area.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, thereby alleviating blockage and promoting ear health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used due to their high concentration of compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate ear blockage.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea angustifolia are often used for their ability to reduce inflammation and fight infection, making them a suitable choice for ear blockage tinctures.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are sometimes used in combination with other parts, due to their potential to aid in reducing swelling and promoting healing in the ear.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried echinacea angustifolia roots from a reliable source and store them in a clean container.
Combine the dried roots with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid from the jar using a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate the solvents from the plant material.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which gently soothe and calm irritated eardrums and ear canals. The tincture's antioxidants also help to reduce swelling and promote healthy blood flow, allowing the ears to clear out excess wax and debris that may be contributing to the blockage. Additionally, maidenhair tree tinctures have natural astringent properties that help to shrink swollen tissues and reduce pain and discomfort associated with ear blockages.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ears, which can contribute to ear blockage.
Vasodilatory effects: The tannins present in Ginkgo biloba can dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the ears and reducing congestion that may be contributing to ear blockage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the ears associated with ear blockage.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the ear, which can cause blockage, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate congestion and reduce the severity of ear blockage.
Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpenoids possess anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow to the ears and alleviate congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used as they contain a compound called ginkgotoxin, which has been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help combat ear infections.
Roots: Ginkgo roots are also used as they contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in the spring or summer when they are in full bloom and the plant is mature.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers with 80 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio by weight in a glass jar.
Steep the ginkgo biloba mixture in the vodka for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its unique properties that promote ear health. The aloe vera plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated ear tissues, reducing swelling and tension that can cause blockages. Additionally, the tincture's antifungal and antibacterial properties help eliminate underlying infections that may be contributing to the blockage. By applying the tincture directly to the affected ear, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and pressure caused by ear blockage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the ear canal, which can contribute to blockages.
Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in aloe vera tincture can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing ear blockage, promoting a healthy ear environment.

Soothing and demulcent properties: Aloe vera's mucilages (thick, gel-like substances) provide a protective barrier for the delicate tissues of the ear canal, soothing irritations and reducing discomfort associated with ear blockage.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with ear blockage.
Catechins: Catechins, a type of flavonoid, exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the ear canal, alleviating blockage.

Aloe-juvabione: Aloe-juvabione, a triterpenoid saponin, has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the ear canal from infections and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for making tinctures due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which can help reduce swelling and promote drainage in the ears.

Gel from Leaves: The clear gel extracted from the leaves is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that can help soothe and calm the ear canal, reducing inflammation and promoting ear drainage.

Sap from Leaves: The sap or latex from the leaves contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and promote ear drainage by reducing swelling and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100% organic fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves 12-18 inches long.
Chop 500 grams of the aloe vera gel into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine 250 milliliters of 80% ethanol with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a large glass jar.
Steep the chopped aloe vera gel in the ethanol water mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with ear blockage because it has a natural decongestant property that efficiently relieves excess mucus buildup in the Eustachian tube. The menthol content in peppermint oil dissolves thickened secretions, allowing for smoother airflow and reduced pressure in the middle ear. As the tincture is absorbed through the mucous membranes, it helps to thin out congestion, making it easier to clear out blockages and restore hearing clarity. This natural remedy provides instant relief from ear discomfort and promotes overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the Eustachian tube, relieving ear blockage.
Expectorant properties: Peppermint's expectorant action helps loosen and clear mucus from the ears, promoting drainage and reducing blockages caused by excess mucus.
Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint's antimicrobial compounds, such as menthone, help combat infections that can cause ear blockage and promote a healthy environment for the middle ear.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: A monoterpene that can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the ear due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Menthol: A terpene that can help thin mucus and provide temporary relief from congestion and blockage in the ear due to its expectorant properties.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the ear due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural decongestant properties.
Stems: The stems are also used to make tinctures, as they contain essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the ears.
Roots: The roots are another part used for tinctures, as they contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the ear canal, reducing pressure and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh leaves of mentha x piperita in the early morning when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves at 35c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and preserve the plant material.
Combine 1 part of the dried leaves with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, resulting in a tincture of 1:5 ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work together to reduce swelling and ease the pressure in the Eustachian tube, allowing it to open up and drain excess mucus. This can help alleviate feelings of fullness or blockage in the ear, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving ear congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be causing ear blockages, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the ear canal, helping to relieve pressure and discomfort associated with blocked ears.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to ear blockages such as tension headaches or sinus congestion.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with ear blockage by reducing inflammation and relieving pain in the ear canal, allowing for proper drainage and healing.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help combat ear infections, which can cause blockage, by killing bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to infection.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the ear canal, allowing for improved drainage and reducing the risk of blockage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and decongest the ears.
Flowers: Their calming and antiseptic properties aid in reducing inflammation and promoting drainage in the ears.
Stems: The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of the stems help to reduce infection and inflammation in the ears, promoting ear health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 part of vodka by weight.
Combine the harvested dried flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with ear blockage because its active compound, glycyrrhizin, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube. When this tube becomes blocked, it can cause pain and discomfort in the ear. By reducing inflammation, herbal licorice tinctures can help alleviate pressure and promote drainage, allowing the tube to function properly again. Additionally, its expectorant properties can help loosen mucus and phlegm, further contributing to the relief of ear blockage symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube and surrounding tissues, helping to relieve ear congestion and blockage.
Soften mucus property: The mucilage content in licorice tinctures helps to loosen and soften excess mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing ear pressure and discomfort.

Antibacterial and antiviral property: Licorice root's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to ear blockage and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have surfactant properties, which can help break down and clear earwax and debris that may be contributing to blockage.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear canal, which may be contributing to blockage.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help thin out earwax and other secretions, making it easier to drain and clear blockages in the ear canal.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps in reducing swelling and promoting drainage in the ears.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate ear congestion and promote healing.
Roots: They are also a source of glycyrrhetic acid, which has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots at a ratio of 1:3 with a solvent in late summer.
Grind 20 grams of the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powder with 60 ml of the solvent in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Filter the liquid through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and congestion in the Eustachian tube. This tube connects the middle ear to the back of the throat and allows for equalization of air pressure, preventing blockages and discomfort. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds also help soothe the mucous membranes, promoting drainage and relieving pressure that can cause ear blockage and related symptoms such as popping, fullness, or muffled hearing.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion tincture helps to reduce water retention and inflammation in the ears, which can contribute to ear blockages by increasing blood flow and reducing swelling.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion tincture help to soothe and calm irritated ear tissues, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by ear blockages.
Expectorant property: Dandelion tincture's expectorant properties help to thin and loosen mucus and debris in the ears, making it easier to drain and clear out any blockages.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: It helps with ear blockage by reducing inflammation and promoting the drainage of mucus from the Eustachian tube, which connects the middle ear to the back of the nose and throat.
Luteolin: It helps with ear blockage by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote the healing of the Eustachian tube.
Quercetin: It helps with ear blockage by acting as a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help reduce swelling and promote the drainage of mucus from the Eustachian tube.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their expectorant properties, which help in loosening and removing mucus from the ears.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing swelling and congestion in the ears.
Flowers: Employed for their antiseptic properties, which help in preventing infections and promoting healing in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Dry the harvested material at 40°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 1 part of dried taraxacum officinale with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the tincture and discard the solids then store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with ear blockage because its antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause congestion and discomfort in the ears. The tincture's natural astringent properties also help to reduce swelling and inflammation, allowing for a clearer and more comfortable listening experience. remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps with ear blockage by thinning mucus and relieving congestion, making it easier to drain excess fluid from the ear.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps relieve ear blockage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves or 2 cups of dried leaves for making tincture.
Combine the harvested leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ears. When used topically, chamomile oil can reduce swelling and ease congestion, allowing for a clearer flow of air through the Eustachian tube and relieving pressure in the middle ear. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or dusk for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dark place for seven days to preserve their therapeutic properties.
Chop 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into small pieces and combine them with 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for eight weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with preparation date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with ear blockage because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube and promote drainage. Additionally, milk thistle's antioxidants help to soothe irritated tissues and calm inflammation in the ear canal, allowing for a clearer passage of sound waves and relief from clogged ears. remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage of mucus and relief from ear blockages.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle tinctures have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections that can cause ear blockage by reducing bacterial or fungal growth in the middle ear.

Silibinin: Silibinin, a bioflavonoid found in milk thistle, has been demonstrated to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the delicate tissues of the ear from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of ear blockage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the roots of milk thistle are effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the ears.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers in their prime, ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum material using a warm air circulation method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, producing 30 grams of herbal material.
Combine the powdered silybum marianum with 90% ethanol in a ratio of 1:2 by weight and steep for 14 days at room temperature.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and filter again using a coffee filter paper, resulting in a final tincture volume of 60 ml.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with ear blockage because it contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the delicate tissues of the ear canal. When applied topically to the affected ear, this gel-like coating helps to reduce inflammation, ease congestion, and promote drainage, allowing for effortless passage of earwax and debris. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures provide effective relief from ear blockage and discomfort, promoting overall hearing health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Emollient and Soothing: Marshmallow tincture's emollient properties help to lubricate and soothe inflamed mucous membranes in the ears, reducing discomfort and congestion caused by ear blockage.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tincture can help reduce swelling and irritation in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage and relief from ear pressure and blockage.

Demulcent and Protective: Marshmallow's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, shielding them from further irritation and inflammation, while its tannins help to stabilize and strengthen the epithelial lining of the Eustachian tube, promoting healthy function and drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in relieving ear congestion and discomfort.
Buds: The buds of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help to reduce inflammation and ease ear blockage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers from pesticide-free sources weighing about 20 grams.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient solvent interaction.
Combine chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5, filling the jar almost completely.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily during this time period to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the plant material and storing the finished product in the refrigerator.

The plant's leaves contain a wealth of compounds that can soothe irritated ear tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be causing the blockage. When taken as a tincture, comfrey's active ingredients are easily absorbed into the bloodstream, allowing them to reach the ears quickly and effectively alleviate symptoms of congestion and ear blockage.

Antioxidant: Comfrey's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate tissues of the ear from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to ear blockage and other hearing-related issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers at peak potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped herb with 1 liter of 95% ethanol in a glass jar, covering the contents completely.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with ear blockage because its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube, a narrow passage connecting the middle ear to the back of the throat. remedy_recipe: Gather dried urtica dioica leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for desired tincture strength.
Combine 1 part dried herb with 2 parts high-proof vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks to allow for steeping time.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids storing them in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Weigh out 10% of the total weight as thymus vulgaris material, using a precise scale for accurate measurements.
Combine the thymus vulgaris material with 80% vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all particles are fully submerged in liquid.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and transferring 20% of the original volume to a smaller container for use.

Chop the fresh leaves into smaller pieces and combine them with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the mixture to steep undisturbed.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the tincture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or 2 cups of dried leaves on a sunny day.

Combine the chopped leaves with 80 proof vodka or 95 percent ethanol in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily.
